summary: PR #2251 decompiled two grpushon functions, grPushOn_802182C8 and fn_80218678, replacing nonmatching placeholders with C implementations and updating fn_80218678's header prototype. The PR body claims a clean ninja build and 100.0 fuzzy_match_percent for all functions.

key_files.role: Implemented grPushOn_802182C8 and fn_80218678; added mp/mplib.h include for mpLib_80057BC0.
Updated fn_80218678 prototype from unknown return/params to bool fn_80218678(void).
evidence_refs: raw_diff: UNK_RET fn_80218678(UNK_PARAMS) changed to bool fn_80218678(void)

what_changed.text: grPushOn_802182C8 now initializes grPushOn_804D6AB8 from Ground_801C49F8, sets stage_info.unk8C.b4 false and b5 true, calls grPushOn_802183E4 for indices 0 and 1, then calls mpLib_80057BC0(0).
fn_80218678 now fetches Ground_801C2BA4(1), null-checks the returned HSD_GObj and its Ground user_data, and returns true only when gp->u.map.xC4_b0 is set; otherwise it returns false.
The header prototype for fn_80218678 was made truthful to the recovered implementation: bool return and void parameters.
The C file added mp/mplib.h to support the recovered mpLib_80057BC0 call.

smart_moves.text: The PR updated the owning header prototype at the same time as recovering fn_80218678, avoiding a stale UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declaration.
The implementation uses typed access through HSD_GObj user_data and Ground's map union arm instead of raw offset or M2C_FIELD-style access.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: HSD_GObj* gobj, Ground* gp, gp->u.map.xC4_b0

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a recovered function body establishes a concrete signature, update the corresponding header declaration rather than leaving UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS behind.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_80218678 implementation returns bool and takes void
raw_diff: grpushon.h changed to bool fn_80218678(void)

decomp_lessons.lesson: Small stage helper matches may depend on direct initialization calls plus flag writes; preserve the observed call order and add the needed subsystem include for newly referenced helpers.
Use typed Ground/HSD_GObj access and explicit null guards when recovering ground-state query helpers if the known structures expose the field being checked.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_80218678 nested null checks and gp->u.map.xC4_b0

naming_conventions.text: The PR kept address-style names for functions without recovered semantic names, such as fn_80218678 and grPushOn_802182C8.
Clear local role names were used for object pointers: gobj for HSD_GObj* and gp for Ground*.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: No special pragmas, register steering, inline assembly, PAD_STACK, or dummy locals appear in the loaded diff. The matching approach shown is straightforward C with typed fields, ordered calls, and header/include cleanup.
The PR body reports verification via clean ninja build and fuzzy_match_percent 100.0 for all functions; no objdiff excerpt or command output is included in the slice.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Recovered bodies should update owning header prototypes
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2251 recovered fn_80218678 as bool fn_80218678(void) and updated src/melee/gr/grpushon.h from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to the concrete prototype in the same change.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Prefer typed Ground fields for simple state queries
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2251 implemented fn_80218678 using HSD_GObj*, Ground* user_data, and gp->u.map.xC4_b0, with null checks, rather than raw offset access.

rejection_notes: Do not generalize the two grPushOn_802183E4 calls in grPushOn_802182C8 into a natural-loop lesson; the PR evidence shows two explicit calls and no discussion of loop attempts.
Do not claim reviewer approval rationale or requested changes; the slice has zero review comments and zero reviews.
